title: PG application layer migration — switch imports to .pg.sql.ts bridge files
consumer_purpose: Enable production code to reference Postgres-variant schema files (.pg.sql.ts) for future PG-native table definitions
claim_atoms: All CLEAN production code files that import from .sql.ts will switch to .pg.sql.ts
All 6 existing .pg.sql.ts bridge files re-export from .sql.ts — no behavior change
1 new bridge file created for sync/event.sql.ts
DIRTY files (session/, storage/, project/, data-migration/, control-plane/) will NOT be touched
adapter.ts dual-write architecture is already sufficient — no changes needed
content:
## PG Application Layer Migration Plan

### Rationale
The codebase already has 6 .pg.sql.ts bridge files that re-export from .sql.ts sources. These provide an import-path abstraction layer: when true PG table definitions are needed, only the .pg.sql.ts files change.

### What changes
1. **Create** `packages/opencode/src/sync/event.pg.sql.ts` — bridge re-exporting EventSequenceTable and EventTable from event.sql
2. **Change imports** in 5 CLEAN files from .sql to .pg.sql:
   - session/projectors.ts:  `./session.sql` → `./session.pg.sql`, `@/control-plane/workspace.sql` → `@/control-plane/workspace.pg.sql`
   - worktree/index.ts:  `../project/project.sql` → `../project/project.pg.sql`
   - account/repo.ts:  `./account.sql` → `./account.pg.sql`
   - share/share-next.ts:  `./share.sql` → `./share.pg.sql`
   - sync/index.ts:  `./event.sql` → `./event.pg.sql`

### What stays
- adapter.ts already has SQLite/PG adapter selection — no DualWriteAdapter needed for this phase
- DIRTY files (session/, storage/, project/, data-migration/, control-plane/) are left untouched
- storage/schema.ts is dirty and stays on .sql imports for now

### Verification
- `bun typecheck` from packages/opencode/
